squeteague.d:
n. An American sciaenoid fish (Cynoscion regalis),
   abundant on the Atlantic coast of the United States, and much valued as
   a food fish. It is of a bright silvery color, with iridescent
   reflections. Called also weakfish, squitee, chickwit, and sea trout.
   The spotted squeteague (C. nebulosus) of the Southern United States is
   a similar fish, but the back and upper fins are spotted with black. It
   is called also spotted weakfish, and, locally, sea trout, and sea
   salmon.

squirts.t:
n. 喷射出的一股液体 ( squirt的名词复数 ); 妄自尊大的年轻人, 不知天高地厚的人
v. （指液体或粉末） 喷出, 喷射( squirt的第三人称单数 )
squirts.d:
n someone who is small and insignificant
n the occurrence of a sudden discharge (as of liquid)
v cause to come out in a squirt
v wet with a spurt of liquid

squish.t:
n. 咯吱声, 挤扁
vt. 把...挤扁, 压扁
vi. 咯吱咯吱地走
squish.d:
n. the noise of soft mud being walked on
v. put (a liquid) into a container or another place by means of a squirting action

squished.t: v. 压扁, 压烂, 咯吱吱地走( squish的过去式 )
squished.d:
v walk through mud or mire
v put (a liquid) into a container or another place by means of a squirting action

squishes.t: v. 压扁, 压烂, 咯吱吱地走( squish的第三人称单数 )
squishes.d:
n the noise of soft mud being walked on
v walk through mud or mire
v put (a liquid) into a container or another place by means of a squirting action

squishier.t: <美俚>湿软的, 粘糊糊的, 易压扁的( squishy的比较级 )

squishiest.t: <美俚>湿软的, 粘糊糊的, 易压扁的( squishy的最高级 )

squishing.t: v. 压扁, 压烂, 咯吱吱地走( squish的现在分词 )
squishing.d:
v walk through mud or mire
v put (a liquid) into a container or another place by means of a squirting action

squishy.t: a. 湿软的, 粘糊糊的
squishy.d: s easily squashed; resembling a sponge in having soft porous texture and compressibility

squit.t: n. 无足轻重的人；废话

squits.t: (squit 的复数) n. 无足轻重的人；废话

squitted.t: (squit 的过去分词) n. 无足轻重的人；废话

squitting.t: (squit 的现在分词) n. 无足轻重的人；废话

squiz.t: n. <澳俚>好奇的一看,好奇的一瞥

squoosh.t:
n. 南瓜, 易压烂的物品, 拥挤的人群, 壁球
v. 压扁, 压制, 镇压, 挤进, 挤压

squop.t:
squop (plural squops)
(tiddlywinks) A wink that is squopped.
squop (third-person singular simple present squops, present participle squopping, simple past and past participle squopped)
(tiddlywinks) To play a wink so that it comes to rest vertically above some or all parts of another wink.
